shortBio: Visual arts student, RMIT University Vietnam, Ho Chi Minh City campus. Specialises in street and documentary photography.
longBio: Tôn Nguyễn grew up in the Bình Thạnh district of Ho Chi Minh City, where the textures of daily life — the markets, the alleys, the faces of neighbours — became his first subjects. Studying Visual Arts at RMIT University Vietnam, he has developed a documentary practice that seeks to honour the dignity of ordinary people navigating an extraordinarily fast-changing city. His work has been exhibited at the RMIT end-of-year show and recognised in the Mekong Photo Prize student category.

shortBio: Visual arts student, RMIT University Vietnam, Hanoi campus. Works across landscape, macro, and cultural documentary photography.
longBio: Ngô Đình Hoàng Phước came to photography through an early fascination with light — its quality, direction, and the way it transforms familiar subjects into something extraordinary. Based at RMIT's Hanoi campus, he has built a body of work that ranges from intimate studies of single flowers to expansive nightscape essays. His series on the lotus ponds of West Lake, begun in his first year of study, has been widely shared and became the foundation for his honored submission to the Cultural Visions archive.
